questions.text: Which of the following is soluble in hot water?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Starch is a mixture of amylose and amylopectin. Amylose makes up about 20% of starch, while amylopectin makes up about 80%. Amylose is soluble in hot water, whereas, amylopectin is not. Since, amylopectin is present in a higher percentage in starch, therefore, starch is not completely soluble in hot water.

Glycogen is a polysaccharide that is similar to amylopectin. It has a branched structure with a- 1,4, glycosidic linkages with branching at a- 1,6, glycosidic linkage. It is a large molecule and like amylopectin is not soluble in hot water.

Amylose is a linear polymer of glucose molecules that are linked together by α-1,4-glycosidic bonds. It is a long, unbranched chain that can form hydrogen bonds with water molecules. This allows amylose to dissolve in hot water.
Amylopectin is a highly branched polymer of glucose molecules that are linked together by α-1,4- and α-1,6-glycosidic bonds. It is a very large molecule that is insoluble in hot and cold water.

questions.explanation: Out of all the options only amylose is soluble in hot water. Amylose is a linear polymer of glucose molecules that are linked together by α-1,4-glycosidic bonds. It is a long, unbranched chain that can form hydrogen bonds with water molecules. This allows amylose to dissolve in hot water. Amylopectin and glycogen are highly branched polymer of glucose molecules that are linked together by α-1,4- and α-1,6-glycosidic bonds. They are very large molecules and are insoluble in hot and cold water. Starch is a mixture of amylose and amylopectin in the ratio of 20% to 80%. As, amylopectin is present in a higher percentage in starch, starch is not completely soluble in hot water.

questions.text: The floor of the chest is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Alveoli are tiny air sacs in your lungs that take up the oxygen you breathe in and keep your body going. Although they're microscopic, alveoli are the workhorses of your respiratory system. People have an average of 480 million alveoli in their lungs, located at the end of bronchial tubes.

Trachea is a long, U-shaped tube that connects your larynx (voice box) to your lungs. The trachea is often called the windpipe. It's a key part of your respiratory system. When you breathe in, air travels from your nose or mouth through your larynx.

The bronchi are the passageways that connect your windpipe to your lungs. You have two main bronchi in your right and left lungs that divide and branch off into smaller segments, like tree branches. At the end of your bronchi, the alveoli exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.explanation: The diaphragm is a muscle that helps you inhale and exhale (breathe in and out). This thin, dome-shaped muscle sits below your lungs and heart. It's attached to your sternum (a bone in the middle of your chest), the bottom of your rib cage and your spine.

questions.text: In seminiferous tubules, repeated division of the germinal epithelium produces?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Oogonia are cells in the female body whereas seminiferous tubules only exist in the male body. Therefore, this is not the correct option.

In the seminiferous tubules of the testes, the repeated division of the germinal epithelium (the cells that give rise to sperm) through a process called spermatogenesis produces sperm cells, also known as spermatozoa. Spermatogenesis is the complex and continuous process by which male germ cells, called spermatogonia, undergo mitotic divisions, meiotic divisions, and maturation to eventually become mature sperm cells. These sperm cells are then released into the lumen of the seminiferous tubules and subsequently travel through the male reproductive system.

questions.text: In human females, the release of the ovum from the follicle is called:
questions.subject: Biology

Fertilization is when the sperm and egg fuses. Therefore, is not the correct option.
Menstruation is monthly vaginal bleeding that is a part of the woman cycle. Thus, this is the correct option.
Implantation is when a fertilized cell attaches to the uterine lining.

questions.explanation: The release of the ovum (egg) from the follicle in human females is called ovulation. Ovulation is a key event in the menstrual cycle and typically occurs around the middle of the menstrual cycle, roughly 14 days before the start of the next menstrual period. During ovulation, a mature follicle in the ovary ruptures, releasing the egg into the fallopian tube, where it can potentially be fertilized by sperm. If fertilization does not occur, the egg will disintegrate, and the menstrual cycle will proceed with the shedding of the uterine lining during menstruation.

questions.text: Darwin's theory mainly focuses on:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Darwin's theory does not focus on the origin of life or how organs become extinct. The origin of life is a complex question that is still not fully understood. There are many different theories about how life first arose on Earth, but none of them have been proven.

The extinction of organs is a relatively rare event. It occurs when an organ becomes no longer necessary for survival. For example, the appendix is a vestigial organ in humans that is no longer necessary for digestion.

Darwin's theory mainly focuses on how new species arise. His theory of evolution by natural selection states that individuals with advantageous traits are more likely to survive and reproduce, passing on their traits to their offspring. Over time, this can lead to the evolution of new species.

Darwin's theory of evolution does not address how organisms form. This is a question that is more closely related to the field of developmental biology. Developmental biology is the study of how organisms develop from a single cell to a complex multicellular organism.

questions.text: This is non-reducing sugar:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Maltose is a reducing sugar. Maltose is a disaccharide that is made up of two glucose molecules linked together by an α-1,4 glycosidic bonds. This bond leaves a free aldehyde group on one of the glucose molecules, making maltose a reducing sugar.

Sucrose is not a reducing sugar. A reducing sugar is a sugar that has a free aldehyde or ketone group. This means that the sugar can be oxidized and can react with other molecules. Sucrose is a disaccharide that is made up of one glucose molecule and one fructose molecule linked together by an α-1,2 glycosidic bonds. This bond does not leave a free aldehyde or ketone group on either of the sugar molecules, making sucrose a non-reducing sugar.

Cellobiose is a reducing sugar. Cellobiose is a disaccharide that is made up of two glucose molecules linked together by a β-1,4 glycosidic bonds. This bond leaves a free aldehyde group on one of the glucose molecules, making cellobiose a reducing sugar.

Lactose is a reducing sugar. Lactose is a disaccharide that is made up of one glucose molecule and one galactose molecule linked together by a β-1,4 glycosidic bonds. This bond leaves a free aldehyde group on the galactose molecule, making lactose a reducing sugar.

questions.explanation: Out of all the options only sucrose is non reducing as it does not have a free aldehyde or ketone group. Maltose, cellobiose and lactose are all reducing sugars.

questions.text: During birth, which of following act as birth canal?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The tube through which an ovum or egg passes from an ovary is called oviduct.

Ovary is one of a pair of female glands in which the eggs form and the female hormones estrogen and progesterone are made. These hormones play an important role in female traits, such as breast development, body shape, and body hair. They are also involved in the menstrual cycle, fertility, and pregnancy.
The hollow, pear-shaped organ in a woman's pelvis is uterus. The uterus is where a fetus (unborn baby) develops and grows.

During birth, the birth canal refers to the pathway through which the baby passes from the uterus to the outside world. In humans, the birth canal is composed of two main structures: Cervix: The cervix is the lower, narrow part of the uterus that connects to the vagina. During labor, the cervix dilates (opens) to allow the baby to pass through. Vagina: The vagina is the muscular tube that extends from the cervix to the external genitalia. It serves as the passageway for the baby during delivery. As labor progresses, contractions of the uterus and the muscles surrounding the birth canal help push the baby through the cervix and into the vagina, ultimately leading to childbirth. The process of labor and delivery is a complex physiological event that involves the coordinated efforts of both the mother's body and the baby's positioning for a successful passage through the birth canal.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the impact of purifying selection?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It increases frequency of an allele.
options.explanation: Increasing the frequency of an allele is the opposite of what purifying selection does. It increases the variation in a population, not the homogeneity.

options.text: It is the same as disruptive selection.
options.explanation: Disruptive selection is a type of natural selection that favors individuals at the extremes of a population's range of variation. This type of selection can lead to the evolution of two or more distinct types of individuals within a population.
Genetic diversity is the total variation in genes present in a population. Purifying selection reduces genetic diversity by removing harmful mutations from a population.

options.text: It removes variation from the population.
options.explanation: Purifying selection is a type of natural selection that removes harmful mutations from a population. Harmful mutations are those that reduce an individual's fitness, or ability to survive and reproduce. Purifying selection can act on any type of mutation, but it is most effective against mutations that have a large impact on fitness.

questions.explanation: The statement that best describes the impact of purifying selection is: "It removes variation from the population." Purifying selection, also known as negative or stabilizing selection, is a type of natural selection that operates to eliminate deleterious or harmful alleles from a population. It favors the preservation of advantageous alleles and the removal of deleterious ones, leading to a reduction in genetic diversity within the population over time. Essentially, purifying selection acts to "purify" the gene pool by removing harmful genetic variants and maintaining or increasing the prevalence of beneficial ones.

questions.text: Hydrogenation of alkenes takes place in the presence of _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogenation of alkenes does not occur in the presence of simple nickel because it does not have the necessary activity and selectivity for hydrogenation reactions.
Hydrogenation of alkenes does not take place in the presence of gold because gold is a noble metal and does not participate in chemical reactions.

Palladium does not catalyze the addition of hydrogen to unsaturated bonds. Palladium is commonly used as a catalyst in other reactions such as the hydrogenation of nitroarenes and carbonyl compounds, but it cannot reduce alkenes to alkanes.

The nickel is a catalyst. The hydrogen interacts with the nickel in a way that weakens the H-H bond, and thus lowers the activation energy for the hydrogenation reaction. The overall energy requirement is the same but instead of doing it in one king-hit, the catalyst permits the activation energy to be achieved in two steps, and the adsorption on the nickel holds the first step in place until the reactant arrives. Hydrogenation of alkenes and alkynes takes place in the presence of certain catalysts. In Sabatier Sendererns's reaction, the addition of hydrogen takes place in the presence of a Raney nickel catalyst.

questions.text: Phenol gives electrophilic substitution reactions due to?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Phenol is an aromatic alcohol, and the presence of a hydroxyl (OH) group in the molecule makes it an electrophilic substrate. The electrophilic character of the hydroxyl group arises due to the high electron density of the oxygen atom, which makes the hydroxyl proton acidic and a good leaving group. This makes the phenol molecule susceptible to electrophilic substitution reactions, where an electrophile can attack the molecule and replace the hydrogen in the hydroxyl group.

questions.explanation: When a transverse wave travels from a rarer medium to a denser medium and encounters a boundary, it undergoes reflection. During reflection, the wave is partially transmitted and partially reflected back into the original medium.If the reflected wave is inverted (180-degree phase shift) compared to the incident wave, the crest of the incident wave will coincide with the trough of the reflected wave. As a result, they will cancel each other out due to destructive interference, and the incident crest will effectively become a trough in the reflected wave.In summary, option c) "An incident crest on reflection becomes a trough" is the correct answer, as a crest becomes a trough upon reflection if the reflected wave is inverted and destructive interference occurs.

questions.text: Pair production is possible in _ photon.

options.explanation: X-rays can undergo scattering when interacting with matter, but they are not typically deflected by electromagnetic fields. Instead, they are absorbed or scattered through processes like Compton scattering or photoelectric effect.

Beta rays consist of high-speed electrons or positrons emitted from a radioactive nucleus. They can be deflected by electric and magnetic fields due to their charge and mass, but they are not typically involved in processes like pair production.

Gamma rays are high-energy photons emitted from radioactive decay or other nuclear processes. They can indeed be deflected by electromagnetic fields. When passing through a strong electric field, they can undergo pair production, where a gamma ray interacts with the field and converts it into an electron-positron pair.

questions.text: Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students?Arguments:(I) Yes. options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because it ignores the potential benefits of providing concessions to certain groups of students. Merit-based admissions can indeed improve the quality of professional institutes, but it does not address the issue of ensuring access and opportunities for underprivileged students.

Option B is incorrect because it overlooks the importance of promoting inclusivity and diversity in professional institutes. Without concessions or affirmative action, socially and economically backward students may face significant barriers to accessing higher education, perpetuating inequalities.

options.text: Both arguments I and II are strong

options.text: Neither argument I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect because both arguments have their strengths.
